mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or pinned near zero, discount rates and recession risk expectations decline, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of observations when FEDFUNDS is declining or below 1%, or rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in CPIAUCSL above recent inflation trend raises expected policy tightening and real discount rates, which should pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL inflation spikes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, so a month-over-month increase in unemployment should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment upshifts are not followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the 3-year rolling IC is consistently near zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS combined with flat or falling UNRATE signals broadening consumer demand without labor market deterioration, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and stable or falling UNRATE does not outperform either series alone, or if the combined signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in a majority of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Strengthening INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and corporate operating leverage; when production is expanding, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal reverses sign during multiple industrial expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below its recent range signals housing demand deterioration and broader cyclical weakness, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if housing breakdowns are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the predictive edge disappears after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stays positive while UNRATE is flat or falling, payroll gains confirm labor-market resilience and support risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-unemployment condition does not beat single-variable signals, or if positive payroll growth with stable unemployment fails to predict positive 20-day returns across rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS paired with above-trend CPIAUCSL should signal policy pressure and multiple compression; this combination should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 0.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the tightening-and-inflation interaction does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.
